Title: Optimization of the fermentation media and growth conditions of Bacillus velezensis BHZ-29 using a Plackett–Burman design experiment combined with response surface methodology
Authors: YingWu Shi, XinXiang Niu, HongMei Yang, Ming Chu, Ning Wang, HuiFang Bao, FaQiang Zhan, Rong Yang, Kai Lou
Quote: A Plackett-Burman design experiment was conducted to determine the significant factors affecting the number of viable bacteria and antibacterial potency

Title: Using design of experiments to guide genetic optimization of engineered metabolic pathways
Authors: Seonyun Moon, Anna Saboe, Michael J Smanski
Quote: Design of experiments (DoE) is a term used to describe the application of statistical approaches to interrogate the impact of many variables on the performance of a multivariate system. Definitive screening designs (DSDs) are a type of fractional factorial design that can estimate main effects as well as the interactions between factors

Title: Optimization of a Bacterial Cultivation Medium via a Design-of-Experiment Approach in a Sartorius Ambr® 15 Fermentation Microbioreactor System
Authors: Antonio Baccante, Pasquale Petruccelli, Giovanni Saudino, Elena Ragnoni, Erik Johansson, Vito Di Cioccio, Kleanthis Mazarakis
Quote: A successful fermentation process depends on the synergy between an efficient bacterial strain and the formulation of a suitable growth medium. Design-of-experiment (DOE) combined with parallel microbioreactor systems enables advancements in fermentation process development
